questions.question: What is the primary goal of the Minimum Distance Classifier in pattern classification by prototype matching?
options: To find the closest matching pattern
To find the farthest non-matching pattern
To find the pattern with the minimum distance
To find the pattern with the maximum distance
questions.explanation: The Minimum Distance Classifier aims to find the pattern that has the minimum distance to the prototype.

questions.question: What is the type of distance measure used in Minimum Distance Classification?
options: Euclidean distance
Manhattan distance
Minkowski distance
Hamming distance
questions.explanation: Euclidean distance is the type of distance measure used in Minimum Distance Classification.

questions.question: What is the assumption made by Minimum Distance Classification?
options: All patterns are similar to the prototype]
All patterns are dissimilar to the prototype
The prototypes are similar to each other
The patterns are similar to the prototypes
questions.explanation: The assumption made by Minimum Distance Classification is that all patterns are dissimilar to the prototype.

questions.question: What is the output of the Minimum Distance Classifier?
options: The index of the closest matching pattern
The index of the farthest non-matching pattern
The similarity measure between the pattern and the prototype
The distance between the pattern and the prototype
questions.explanation: The output of the Minimum Distance Classifier is the index of the farthest non-matching pattern.

questions.question: What is the result of the Minimum Distance Classifier when all patterns are matched to the prototype?
options: All patterns are classified as similar
All patterns are classified as dissimilar
The classifier is unable to classify the patterns
The classifier outputs a tie score
questions.explanation: When all patterns are matched to the prototype, the Minimum Distance Classifier classifies all patterns as similar.
